title: pgx Pooling, Query Contracts, Transactions, and Cancellation · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded pgx repository and unit-of-work layer and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Create one bounded pgxpool, verify startup deliberately, observe acquire wait, and close after admission and worker shutdown.
Use parameterized typed queries, explicit columns, row closure, iteration errors, cardinality expectations, and explainable mapping.
Run every unit-of-work statement on one transaction, defer rollback safely, commit once, and preserve the original cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Pool Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 9198 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove pgx pool, rows, one-connection transactions, cancellation, and error ownership; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one bounded pgxpool, verify startup deliberately, observe acquire wait, and close after admission and worker shutdown.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 6865 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove pgx pool, rows, one-connection transactions, cancellation, and error ownership; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify constraint, serialization, deadlock, cancellation, timeout, connection, and unknown failures without leaking SQL or secrets.
